normalization_point: K-food Green stays possible, but export, ASP, OPM, reorder, inventory, and safety checks must align.
Hero products can create Stage 2, but Stage 3 needs repeat portfolio evidence and safety guardrails.
Global K-food portfolio evidence is stronger than one viral product, but sell-through and inventory still decide quality.
Viral culture is Stage 1 routing evidence until sell-through, reorder, and OPM prove recurrence.
K-beauty Green needs offline sell-through and repeat orders, not TikTok or channel entry alone.
Offline channel entry is Stage 2; sell-through and reorder are the Stage 3 proof.
K-beauty retail platform evidence is Stage 1-2 until store-level sell-through, reorder, inventory, and FCF are visible.
K-beauty export growth must pass tariff, import-review, price-increase, and gross-margin-buffer checks.
Beauty devices can be strong, but device ASP, unit sales, safety, and 4B risk must be checked.
Affiliate commerce can route candidates, but CAC, discounts, creator reliance, and reorder decide margin quality.
Beauty devices need safety and regulatory gates when electrical, medical-grade, or clinical claims appear.
Beauty OEM/ODM can be Green-capable, but customer inventory and receivables are hard checks.
Fast beauty product velocity is a risk overlay when SKU churn, inventory, or receivables outrun sell-through.
Convenience retail is Watch-to-Green only when store productivity drives OPM and FCF.
E-commerce scale is not Green evidence unless unit economics, FCF, security, and supplier regulation are clean.
Fresh e-commerce must prove unit economics and waste control; GMV or listing hopes are Stage 1 only.
Apparel and fast fashion are Watch/Red because IP, safety, tariff, and inventory can break the thesis.
Ultra-low-cost cross-border platforms are RedTeam-first because product safety, IP, customs, and platform regulation dominate.
Fast-fashion IP and supplier litigation are RedTeam overlays, not positive growth evidence.
Fast-fashion product safety and DSA enforcement can cap or break low-cost platform narratives.
Home appliance Green needs rental/service recurring economics, not hardware replacement sales.
Hardware appliance cycles are RedTeam-first unless rental/service recurrence dominates hardware replacement demand.
Kids/home education is RedTeam-first because demographics and TAM can cap the rerating.
Regulated consumer products stay Watch until approval becomes legal recurring revenue.
Food safety is a RedTeam overlay, not a positive score bucket.
E-commerce trust and security are hard gates; customer count and logistics scale do not offset a trust break.
Shipment and channel entry must be separated from sell-through and reorder.
E-commerce OPM quality must distinguish logistics efficiency from supplier pressure or delayed payments.
Discount and affiliate promotion are RedTeam gates when sales growth comes from margin-damaging promotion.
Stage 3 is capped when export, channel, inventory, reorder, or margin detail is missing.
